overview: Green Eldrazi in Modern leverages land recovery and mana acceleration to deploy massive creatures ahead of curve. The deck focuses on recovering lands from the graveyard to maintain tempo and overwhelm opponents with raw power. Signature cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker enable this aggressive ramp strategy.
win_conditions: Deploying large Eldrazi creatures early via mana acceleration
Overwhelming opponents with combat damage from oversized threats
Maintaining board presence through continuous land recovery

keep_criteria: Hands with early land recovery spells like Kozilek's Command
Playable one-drops or early land recoverers on curve
Sufficient early land drops (4+ cards in hand)
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably Forests or duals), plus at least one early disruption (Thought-Knot Seer) or ramp spell.
Keep hands with 3+ mana sources and a Thought-Knot Seer or Kozilek's Command.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking land recovery or acceleration
Hands with only high-cost threats and no early plays
Hands with too many lands and no spells
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, multiple copies of the same low-cost spell without lands, or hands lacking early interaction against artifact/combo decks.

early_game: Play lands and immediately use land recovery spells to accelerate
Deploy early threats or Thought-Knot Seer to establish board presence
Prioritize mana efficiency to reach turn three or four drops
Turn 1: Play a Forest, cast Thought-Knot Seer if possible, or hold for ramp.
Turn 2: Cast ramp spells (Cultivate/Farseek) or drop a second land, then cast Thought-Knot Seer if mana allows.
Prioritize playing lands first, then casting disruption/ramp.
If you have a hand with Kozilek's Command, hold for turn 3-4 when you can cast it for its full value.
mid_game: Continue recovering lands to maintain tempo against midrange decks
Play medium-sized Eldrazi to apply consistent pressure
Use Kozilek's Command for removal or card advantage when ahead
Balance mana for ramp vs. casting threats.
Use Thought-Knot Seer's scry/discard to dig for answers or disrupt opponent's combo pieces.
Manage graveyard by avoiding unnecessary scry if opponent runs graveyard hate, but generally, Eldrazi benefits from card advantage.
Commit threats when you have removal/hate ready for their interaction.
Hold back against sweepers or board wipes until you have a way to protect or recast.
late_game: Deploy massive Eldrazi threats to close out the game
Focus on combat damage rather than card draw
Use remaining land recovery to stabilize against control answers
Glaring Fleshraker (trample, haste, artifact/enchantment destruction), Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, or World Breaker.
Get there by ramping to 4-5 mana, using disruption to clear the path, and deploying threats when opponents are tapped out or out of answers.
Go all-in when you have a lethal threat on board, opponent has no sweepers or targeted removal, or you can follow up with a second threat.
Also commit when you've disrupted their combo pieces early.

key_cards.name: Thought-Knot Seer
key_cards.role: Early land recovery and board presence
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to recover a land from the graveyard, accelerating into larger threats while providing a body to block or attack.

key_cards.name: Kozilek's Command
key_cards.role: Versatile two-drop for acceleration, removal, or value
key_cards.notes: Use the land recovery mode early to ramp, switch to removal or draw when ahead, and generate tokens to protect larger threats.

key_cards.name: Glaring Fleshraker
key_cards.role: Land recovery engine and early threat
key_cards.notes: Return lands to hand to replay them for untapped mana, enabling faster casting of Eldrazi while maintaining a 2/2 body on the battlefield.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize land recovery on turns one and two to enable three and four mana threats
Hold up mana for potential counterspells if facing control, otherwise play threats on opponent's turn
Use Kozilek's Command removal to clear blockers before attacking with large Eldrazi

Common mistakes: Overcommitting to the board without answering their interaction, misusing Kozilek's Command (always pick the artifact/enchantment mode against this metagame), and forgetting to scry with Thought-Knot Seer to dig for answers.
Unique interaction: Glaring Fleshraker's ETB can clear key artifacts/enchantments, making it a perfect mid-game swing against this specific metagame.

matchups.advice: Race their hand size and use land recovery to play threats ahead of curve
Use early land recovery to deploy large blockers and finish quickly
Maintain tempo with continuous land recovery and apply steady pressure

Eldrazi threats are typically uncounterable or cast via ramp that bypasses counters. Use Thought-Knot Seer to discard counterspells. Cast Kozilek's Command on their permanents before they can respond.
Out-ramp them with your own acceleration. Use Thought-Knot Seer to disrupt their mana rocks. Kozilek's Command can remove key ramp pieces.

Win by out-drawing and out-resolving. Thought-Knot Seer is key to disrupting their hand. Prioritize clearing their threats with Kozilek's Command. Win the resource war by ramping faster and deploying uncounterable threats.

common_threats: Watch for artifact acceleration (Arcbound Ravager, Mycosynth Lattice), combo win conditions (Storm count, Blink triggers), and sweepers (Wrath of God, Day of Judgment).
Note when opponents tap out for spells vs. holding mana for protection.
